questions.text: Accounting sees duplicate vendor invoices with slightly different bank info. Indicator?

options.text: Normal ERP rounding, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Possible vendor fraud or BEC—verify via known contacts
Network beaconing, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Antivirus update, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Bank detail changes on invoices are classic fraud indicators.
questions.concept: Pattern analytics and verification stop gift-card, invoice, and vendor fraud schemes.

questions.text: Expense reports spike for gift cards before holidays. Control?

options.text: Publish all card numbers, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Auto-approve all under $1M, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Manager attestation and analytics for outlier patterns
Disable finance department, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Gift card fraud is pattern-detectable with analytics.
questions.concept: Pattern analytics and verification stop gift-card, invoice, and vendor fraud schemes.

questions.text: Whistleblower reports kickbacks in procurement. Security role?

options.text: Delete emails to reduce clutter, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable firewalls, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Post names on intranet, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Preserve digital evidence logs and support forensic imaging per legal

questions.explanation: Fraud investigations need preserved logs and coordinated legal hold.
questions.concept: Pattern analytics and verification stop gift-card, invoice, and vendor fraud schemes.

questions.text: Payroll adds a new bank account for an employee who did not request a change. Indicator?

options.text: Normal behavior when employees travel internationally for conferences, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Possible payroll diversion fraud requiring HR verification before payment
Routine HR system bug that should be ignored if pay amount is unchanged
Evidence that multifactor authentication on email is unnecessary for finance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Unrequested payroll account changes are a classic fraud indicator.
questions.concept: Pattern analytics and verification stop gift-card, invoice, and vendor fraud schemes.

questions.text: An admin can post and approve journal entries alone. Issue?

options.text: Segregation of duties violation enabling financial statement fraud
Improved efficiency only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
HIPAA mandate, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Required for SOC 2 always, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: SoD requires separate roles for posting vs approval.
questions.concept: Segregation of duties and documented journal entries deter financial manipulation.

questions.text: Month-end adjustments lack supporting documentation in the share. Risk?

options.text: No risk if CFO approves verbally
Faster close process only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Better encryption, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Undetected manipulation of financial results

questions.explanation: Audit trails and documentation underpin accounting integrity.
questions.concept: Segregation of duties and documented journal entries deter financial manipulation.

questions.text: GL export to Excel for 'analysis' bypasses ERP controls. Mitigation?

options.text: Restrict exports and use controlled reporting cubes with logging
Email GL to all staff, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Share one password for finance, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable ERP, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Uncontrolled exports undermine integrity of source system.
questions.concept: Segregation of duties and documented journal entries deter financial manipulation.

questions.text: Auditors find journal entries posted after close without approval workflow. Issue?

options.text: Breaks segregation of duties and enables post-close financial manipulation
Only a problem if the company is publicly traded on major exchanges
Improves agility because accountants can fix numbers faster without delays, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Required for real-time dashboards and therefore always acceptable, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Post-close entries need the same approval and audit trail as regular entries.
questions.concept: Segregation of duties and documented journal entries deter financial manipulation.

questions.text: Wire transfers over $50k require two approvals in policy but ERP allows one. Fix?

options.text: Remove approvals for speed, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Use personal Venmo, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Configure system-enforced dual approval workflow matching policy
Lower threshold to $5M, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Technical enforcement must mirror financial policy.
questions.concept: Dual approval on banking and master-data changes blocks process abuse.

questions.text: AP clerks can create vendors and pay them. BEST SoD change?

options.text: Give everyone Domain Admin, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Separate vendor master maintenance from payment initiation roles
Disable logging, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Allow shared clerk login, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Creating vendors and paying is high-risk combined role.
questions.concept: Dual approval on banking and master-data changes blocks process abuse.

questions.text: Automated ACH file generation has no integrity check before upload. Add?

options.text: Disable bank callbacks, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Cryptographic signing or hash verification with dual review of file totals
Email ACH files to personal accounts, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Run at 3 a.m. without logs, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Integrity controls prevent tampered payment files.
questions.concept: Dual approval on banking and master-data changes blocks process abuse.

questions.text: Wire transfers under $25k auto-approve if initiated by finance managers. Risk?

options.text: Required by PCI-DSS for all card-not-present transactions globally, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Fraudsters can split large thefts into smaller wires bypassing dual control thresholds
Improves security because smaller amounts are never targeted by criminals, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Eliminates the need for out-of-band verification on any payment change, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Threshold-based auto-approval enables structuring; use consistent dual control.
questions.concept: Dual approval on banking and master-data changes blocks process abuse.

questions.text: PDF invoice totals do not match ERP line items but bank account matches a new vendor. Suspicion?

options.text: Invoice manipulation fraud—halt payment and verify vendor
Normal OCR error only, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
DNS tunneling, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Patch Tuesday, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Mismatch between documents and master data signals fraud.
questions.concept: Look-alike domains and changed remittance details need vendor verification.

questions.text: Attacker inserts a fake line item via compromised AP mailbox rules. Detection?

options.text: Hunt for hidden forwarding rules and compare invoice hashes to PO
Ignore mailbox rules, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable SPF, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.
Pay faster, using shared credentials instead of individual accountability.

questions.explanation: Mailbox rules are common in invoice fraud; PO matching catches changes.
questions.concept: Look-alike domains and changed remittance details need vendor verification.

questions.text: Vendor portal credentials leaked. Financial control to activate?

options.text: Force portal password reset and callback verification for any bank changes
Stop using vendors, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Disable two-person review, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Publish new password in email, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Portal compromise enables payment redirect fraud.
questions.concept: Look-alike domains and changed remittance details need vendor verification.

questions.text: PDF invoice metadata shows different authoring software than prior invoices from the vendor. Action?

options.text: Post the PDF on the intranet so AP can crowdsource authenticity checks, without security or identity team coordination per policy.
Hold payment and verify with vendor through established contacts; hunt for BEC
Trust the invoice if the total matches the purchase order within ten percent
Pay immediately because PDF files cannot be altered once exported, without security or identity team coordination per policy.

questions.explanation: Metadata anomalies may signal forged invoices in BEC campaigns.
questions.concept: Look-alike domains and changed remittance details need vendor verification.
